name: plan-why-build-this
title: Product Brief
description: Use when turning a rough idea into a short product brief that names the user, problem, intended outcome, success signals, and non-goals before writing specs or design docs. Do not use for detailed feature requirements or technical solution design.
purpose: Capture why the work matters, for whom it matters, and what success looks like before deeper specification begins.
when_to_use: Need a high-level brief before feature spec or design work.
Need to make audience, problem, success signals, and non-goals explicit.
Need one short alignment document that prevents premature solution design.
do_not_use: Need detailed functional requirements or edge-case behavior.
Need navigation structure or information hierarchy.
Need technical boundaries, data flow, or implementation trade-offs.

required_inputs.name: PROBLEM_STATEMENT
required_inputs.description: Plain-language statement of the problem worth solving.

required_inputs.name: TARGET_AUDIENCE
required_inputs.description: Primary users or stakeholder groups and the context they are in.

required_inputs.name: DESIRED_OUTCOMES
required_inputs.description: Intended outcomes and why each one matters.

required_inputs.name: SUCCESS_SIGNALS
required_inputs.description: Observable signals or metrics that indicate the brief succeeded.

required_inputs.name: CONSTRAINTS
required_inputs.description: Explicit time, policy, compatibility, resource, or non-goal constraints.

required_inputs.name: KNOWN_NON_GOALS
required_inputs.description: Items that should stay out of scope even if adjacent to the problem.

input_contract_notes: PROBLEM_STATEMENT should stay problem-first and avoid embedding a preferred solution.
TARGET_AUDIENCE should be specific enough that a later spec can make trade-offs for real users rather than a generic 'everyone'.
SUCCESS_SIGNALS should be observable. If exact metrics are unknown, use proxy signals rather than aspirational language.
KNOWN_NON_GOALS should be explicit when adjacent asks are likely to create scope creep.

structured_outputs.name: BRIEF_SUMMARY
structured_outputs.description: One short summary of the problem, target user, and intended outcome.

structured_outputs.name: USER_JOBS
structured_outputs.description: User jobs and pain points implied by the brief.

structured_outputs.name: OUTCOME_PRIORITIES
structured_outputs.description: Ranked outcomes and the signal that will show whether each one improved.

structured_outputs.name: OPEN_ASSUMPTIONS
structured_outputs.description: Assumptions that later spec or validation work must confirm.

output_contract_notes: BRIEF_SUMMARY should stay short enough that it can anchor later spec work without becoming a long concept document.
USER_JOBS should describe user needs, not solution ideas dressed up as needs.
OUTCOME_PRIORITIES should rank what matters most instead of treating every goal as equally critical.
OPEN_ASSUMPTIONS should stay explicit rather than being buried in prose.
neutrality_rules: Separate user problem from preferred solution.
Do not invent business impact or certainty that the inputs do not support.
Keep non-goals and open assumptions visible instead of implying universal buy-in.
execution_constraints: Do not turn this skill into a detailed feature spec or technical design document.
Prefer the smallest brief that still makes user, outcome, and scope boundaries explicit.
If the audience or success signal is unclear, surface it as an assumption rather than guessing silently.
default_program:
[stages: preflight>detect>analyze>plan>review>handoff>audit |
 scope: repo|paths(glob,...) |
 policy: evidence,quality-gates{docs,compat},deterministic-output |
 lens: christensen-jtbd |
 output: md(contract=v1)]
example_invocation:
$plan-why-build-this
BRIEF_SCOPE: feature
PROBLEM_STATEMENT: 신규 사용자가 첫 로그인 후 설정 화면에서 길을 잃는다
TARGET_AUDIENCE:
  - USER: 신규 관리자
    CONTEXT: 팀 워크스페이스 첫 설정 직후
DESIRED_OUTCOMES:
  - OUTCOME: 첫 설정 완료 시간을 줄인다
    WHY_IT_MATTERS: 초기 이탈을 줄여야 한다
SUCCESS_SIGNALS:
  - SIGNAL: 첫 설정 완료율 증가
    HOW_MEASURED: 7일 cohort 완료율
lens_rationale: A product brief should clarify the user job, pain, and desired progress before the team writes detailed requirements or solution documents.
